output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Demand It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your deposit? Demand it back in 60 seconds for $19.99. No signup needed. Generate your letter now at Deposits-Back.com.
content_outline: Top Illegal Reasons Landlords Use to Withhold Deposits
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Landlords Actually Have
Normal Wear and Tear vs. Actual Damage: Know the Difference
Why a Formal Demand Letter Works Better Than Texts and Calls
How to Escalate to Small Claims Court If Your Landlord Ignores You
FAQ: Can My Landlord Keep My Deposit for Breaking the Lease or Cleaning?
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days and provide an itemized deduction list.
Landlords cannot legally deduct for normal wear and tear, routine cleaning, or made-up charges.
A written demand letter citing state law is the fastest, most effective step before filing in small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com generates a state-specific, legally sound demand letter in under 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 fee with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription. Visit Deposits-Back.com now.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters who have moved out and are experiencing wrongful security deposit withholding, non-return, or unfair deductions by their landlord.
